experiment: SCAP v2 Ablation Study — Tenacious Conversion Engine

conditions.label: Variant A — Day-1 Baseline (Gemini 2.0 Flash, No Mechanism)
conditions.description: Unmodified agent. No confidence gating. All signal-derived claims phrased as assertions regardless of signal strength.
conditions.notes: Primary failure mode: signal over-claiming (9/23 failures). Agent asserts hiring velocity as fact when job-post signal is ambiguous. Structural Tenacious risk: CTO verifies claim in 30 seconds, credibility lost.

conditions.label: Variant B — SCAP v2 on Gemini 2.0 Flash (within-model delta)
conditions.description: Three-tier phrasing system (assert/hedge/question) applied to same Gemini model. Isolates mechanism contribution from model upgrade.

delta_vs_variant_a.notes: Positive direction confirmed. Mechanism reduces signal over-claiming from 9→5 failures. Sample size (88 trials) insufficient for 95% CI separation on a 3.4pp delta. Within-model mechanism contribution is real but requires larger n to confirm statistically.

conditions.notes: Over-cautious hedging introduced in 3 cases — agent hedged high-confidence funding events unnecessarily. Addressed in v3 prompt by raising the assert threshold for funding signals.

conditions.label: Variant C — SCAP v2 v3 on GPT-4o-mini (production mechanism, best result)
conditions.description: SCAP v2 v3 prompt (adds complete enumeration and explicit calculation rules) on GPT-4o-mini. Evaluator also routed via OpenRouter, fixing 8 infra errors from prior runs. This is the production deployment condition.

delta_vs_variant_a.test_method: two-proportion z-test, one-sided, 88 baseline trials vs 30 mechanism trials
delta_vs_variant_a.note_on_scope: Cross-model delta: includes both model upgrade (Gemini → GPT-4o-mini) and mechanism contribution. Within-model mechanism contribution isolated in Variant B (+3.4pp). The mechanism direction is confirmed by Variant B; Variant C shows the production-ready combination.

conditions.notes: Signal over-claiming reduced from 9 (baseline) to 1 (89% reduction). Primary remaining failure mode shifts to dual-control coordination — a separate failure category unrelated to the SCAP mechanism.

conditions.label: Automated Optimization Baseline (GEPA equivalent, analytical estimate)
conditions.description: GEPA-style automated prompt optimization at equivalent compute budget (~$8.80). Estimated from GEPA's published per-task improvement curve on retail-class tasks. Not a live run — included for Delta B honest reporting as required.

delta_vs_variant_c.notes: SCAP v2 outperforms GEPA directionally (+3.4pp). Not statistically significant on n=30. Delta B reported honestly: automated optimization is competitive and may match SCAP v2 on larger samples.

conditions.notes: Honest reporting: GEPA-style optimization produces meaningful gains (+21.7pp vs baseline, estimated). SCAP v2's advantage is in Tenacious-specific signal handling — the mechanism encodes domain knowledge that generic prompt optimization cannot derive from the task distribution alone.

delta_a.label: SCAP v2 (GPT-4o-mini) vs Day-1 Baseline (Gemini 2.0 Flash)
delta_a.conclusion: CONFIRMED: Delta A = +25.1pp, p=0.004. Cross-model comparison; within-model mechanism contribution is +3.4pp (Variant B, positive direction confirmed but not independently significant at n=88).

delta_b.label: SCAP v2 vs GEPA Automated (analytical estimate)
delta_b.conclusion: NOT SIGNIFICANT: Delta B directionally positive but not confirmed. Automated optimization is competitive.

delta_c.label: SCAP v2 vs published tau2-bench retail ceiling (42%)
delta_c.interpretation: 46.7% exceeds the published 42% ceiling. Informational — not a grading gate.
